THIS WEEK IN LOCAL GOVERNMENT

Kronenwetter launched a new Parks Committee with plans for a major playground overhaul at Bushka Park, while the Wausau School Board and the teachers union opened contract talks for the coming school year.

  • Kronenwetter's newly formed Parks Committee elected Erin Mishka as chair and began planning a $150,000–$200,000 playground replacement at Bushka Park, with a July 17 movie-under-the-stars fundraiser in the works.
  • The Wausau Board of Education and the Wausau Education Association exchanged initial contract proposals for the 2026-27 school year at a July 1 public meeting.
  • Kronenwetter's Parks Committee scheduled a public parks tour for Wednesday, July 8 at 4 p.m. at the municipal center, with July 9 as the rain date.
  • Kronenwetter's CLIPP Committee is set to vote on revisions to Chapter 382 of its nuisance ordinances and consider traffic control measures on County Road XX and Terrebonne Drive.

Coming up: The busy week ahead includes Weston's Village Board of Trustees and Community Life & Public Safety Committee both meeting Monday, along with Wausau's Economic Development Committee on Tuesday and the Marathon County Infrastructure Committee on Thursday.

Full summaries on the Meeting Tracker →
